hawkish_phrases: No participants anticipated that it would be appropriate to begin reducing the federal funds rate target in 2023
a restrictive policy stance would need to be maintained until the incoming data provided confidence that inflation was on a sustained downward path to 2 percent, which was likely to take some time
historical experience cautioned against prematurely loosening monetary policy
all participants had raised their assessment of the appropriate path of the federal funds rate relative to their assessment at the time of the September meeting
dovish_phrases: slowing in the pace of rate increases at this meeting would better allow the Committee to assess the economy's progress
inflation data received for October and November showed welcome reductions in the monthly pace of price increases
tentative signs of labor market imbalances improving
core goods prices declined in the October and November CPI data
measures of rent based on new leases were indicating a deceleration
summary: The FOMC unanimously raised rates by 50bp to 4.25-4.50%, slowing the pace from prior meetings, but signaled ongoing increases ahead with no cuts anticipated in 2023 and all participants raising their expected rate path, maintaining a clearly hawkish stance despite acknowledging some early progress on inflation.
